Key Takeaways:
• Types of Evergreen Launches:
o Rolling Launch
▪ On a periodic basis you find JV’s and you recruit them to mail into your
sequence
▪ Works well with webinars - once they’re on the list you mail them to the
webinar and the replay
▪ If certain JV’s can’t mail for you during the launch, you can ask them to
mail into your squeeze page that leads into this webinar
▪ Or you could run these leads into your exact launch again
▪ One benefit of this (as a JV) is that there’s much less competition - no
need to compete with other JV partners
o Pure Evergreen
▪ Set up a squeeze page
▪ Traffic streams lead into the squeeze page - social media, SEO, PPC,
media, or JV partners
▪ There should be a delay of about one week before leading them to your
PLC’s and Sales Video
▪ There are a few different ways to do this…
• You can have the launch running all the time and have it based on
when they opt in
• You can do a weekly launch where you gather all the leads from
that week and do a launch - this can be done on a monthly basis
too
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 8 --

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• When you get to the monthly or longer level, you’ll run into the
previously mentioned issue with paid traffic - they may not be
relevant anymore at that time
▪ Paid traffic works well for Evergreen that is always on or weekly
o Another method:
▪ Set up a squeeze page
▪ Run traffic sources to it
▪ Set up a webinar - the content that’s delivered here is usually the same as
your PLC’s, but much shorter and boiled down - the offer also happens
here
▪ 70-90 minutes long with last 10-20 minutes for the offer
▪ This then leads to a sales page (usually not a video) - just a headline and
an offer
▪ Usually around 30-40% of people that opt in attend the webinar
▪ You need a sequence of emails to get people to show up
o You have to be careful about the webinar aspect - there are ways to work around
this to make it seem live rather than pre-recorded
▪ This can be deceptive and you should be careful because people can find
out pretty easily
▪ The deceptiveness could be detrimental to your brand/business
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 8 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now I want to talk about the different types of Evergreen product launches. We
call the first one the “rolling launch.” This is very simple. It works really well with
webinars.
There are a couple of different ways you do that. On a periodic basis, you find
joint venture partners. You go out and recruit them to mail into your sequence. You can
do this a couple of different ways.
You can just have a webinar. Then you will have a squeeze page. How am I going
to fit the word “webinar” in there? Let’s put a “W” there for webinar.
They go to JVs. Here’s your JV. They mail into your squeeze page. You do a
webinar for those folks.
After the webinar, there’s a replay and some e-mails that you’ll send. These
people get on the list. You mail the list the webinar. You mail them the replay a couple of
times. This can be very effective.
The cool thing about this is that you can do a regular launch. There will always be
some JVs who can’t play along or participate. Maybe they have their internal launch
going.
Maybe they’re involved in another launch. Maybe you don’t develop a
relationship with them until well after the launch. This is really simple. You just need a
squeeze page and a webinar. Bingo, you’re there.
It doesn’t take much at all. If you’re in a discussion with this JV and they can’t
participate or your meet them after your launch, say, “How about you mail out for my
webinar?”
You have to go back and do all of the JV stuff I talked about early on. You build
relationships. You can promote for them first. This is all the JV relationship stuff I
covered earlier in the course. You have to do that.
When it’s time to ask them to promote, you can simply ask them to mail for your
webinar or you could run them into your launch again. Here’s your JV. They’re mailing
into your squeeze page.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 8 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
You have your Prelaunch Content, another Prelaunch Content, another Prelaunch
Content and your sales letter or sales video. You can have them mail for your squeeze
page there.
Maybe you can get them to mail multiple times. This one is going into the first
piece of Prelaunch Content. Maybe they’ll mail for another squeeze page that goes into
the second piece of Prelaunch Content.
Maybe they’ll mail for the squeeze page that goes into the third piece of Prelaunch
Content. Some JVs will do this for you and some won’t. It’s all negotiable, of course.
Maybe they’ll even mail into your sales letter.
I’ve done this and had JVs mailing for me. I’ve also done it for other JVs where
I’m mailing into their sequence. It’s worked really well.
One nice thing as a joint venture partner when I do this is that I’m not competing
with a lot of other people. When I think of it as a JV, if I mail to one of these, I’m not
competing with other JV partners. I don’t have to put together some massive bonus
package.
I don’t have to worry about mailing before the other partners. Doing something
like this can be attractive to joint venture partners. When you’re trying to sell them on it
to convince them to promote for you, you can point that out.
You can say, “Hey, you don’t have to compete with any other affiliates or
partners. We’re doing this just for you.” You can either do it using your Prelaunch
Content or you can do it via a webinar. It can be really effective.
That’s the rolling launch. It’s the most simple. You go on to individual partners
and convince them to mail for you. That’s fairly old school. I’ve been teaching launches
for a long time. I started teaching that in 2005.
There’s another way you can do it. This is more pure Evergreen. This is where you
have a squeeze page and you have traffic streams into that squeeze page.
These traffic streams could be any number of traffic streams. They could be social
media. They could be SEO. They could be paid pay-per-click with AdWords. They could
be banners.
They could be Facebook. They could be YouTube. They could be joint venture
partners. They come into the squeeze page.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Generally, there is some delay in here. I have a new blue marker to show you
here. There is usually some delay. It could be a couple of days, maximum of a week.
Then they get the first piece of Prelaunch Content. Then you lead them right
through the sequence. Then there is a sales letter. It’s that simple.
There’s not a lot to it other than the details and mechanics which we will get to in
a little bit. You have a few different ways you can do this. You can have this like it’s
running all the time.
If someone comes in on June 1st, then they get the first piece on June 4th. If they
come in on June 2nd, then they get this piece on June 5th and so on. That’s pure
Evergreen. It’s 100% based on when they opt in.
Another way you can do this is to do a weekly launch. All of the leads coming in
on a given week, the next week, they start a launch. You can do this on a monthly basis as
well. You can even do it on a quarterly basis.
If you’re doing something like the monthly or quarterly basis or any sequence you
want, as you get to monthly or longer, you get into that problem with the paid traffic I
talked about in the last video where you’re spending a lot of money on traffic sources.
You don’t know if they’re going to convert.
You’re investing a whole lot of money and you’re going negative in your business.
You don’t know how it’s going to work. For paid traffic, it works really well if this is
always turned on or if it’s weekly. If the sequences get out much further than that, then
it’s a bit of a problem. That’s sequence based.
The other way to go Evergreen with this is where you have a squeeze page, your
traffic sources and then you go for the old webinar. We’re going to have to do this a little
bit different here, folks. Let’s go with a different symbol for webinar.
This method is working very well right now for a lot of people. You have a squeeze
page. The squeeze page offer is, “Leave your name and e-mail address. We have this
webinar coming up.” You drive them into a webinar.
The webinar sends them to a sales page. This is usually not a video. The real sales
pitch happens on the webinar. The webinar could be anywhere from 70 to 90 minutes
long.
You’re giving some content. The content is similar to what you would give in your
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 8 --

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
pre- launch. If you go through the whole Prelaunch story and the content you’re going to
give, now you have to boil that down and go from being three 18 minute videos to a 60-
minute webinar followed up with a 10, 15 or 20-minute pitch.
That’s where we get the 70 to 90 minutes. The webinar is your Prelaunch
Content. It’s the entire sequence put into a webinar followed up with a pitch. The sales
page is more like an order page.
You reiterate the offer. It’s a headline and an offer. Not a heck of a lot more than
that. It’s not a big long sales video. It’s not a big long sales letter. The bulk of the pitch
comes on the webinar in the final 10 to 20 minutes.
I was going to write down “mins”, spelled M-I-N-S as an abbreviation. I almost
said “mins.” That sounded a little weird. I thought, “OK, I’ll just say minutes.”
The last 10 to 20 minutes is the pitch. You can take the material that you’ve done
in terms of putting together your Prelaunch story and the Prelaunch videos. Take those
and develop a PowerPoint presentation. That goes on the webinar.
One of the important considerations here is driving the maximum. When you go
from the squeeze page, what’s the show-up rate? Industry wide, all webinars across
niches get about 30%, 35%, 36%, 37% or maybe 40%. Somewhere between 30% to 40%
of the people who opt in actually show up.
You have to sell folks even after they’ve opted in. You have a sequence here of e-
mails. At the bare minimum, you have an e-mail that goes out right away that says,
“Thanks for opting in. This is going to be the coolest webinar ever.” Then you need a 24-
hour notice. Then you need something to go out an hour before. Sometimes even after
the webinar has started you can send another e-mail.
This webinar process can be completely automated. People have completely
automated it. It works really well. In fact, it’s not a webinar at all. You send people to a
video that seems like a webinar.
You can do it that way or you can do live webinars. When you do a webinar, since
it’s an event, it tends to be more impactful than sending someone to a video. You say,
“Hey, watch my video of a webinar.”
There is a whole way of making it seems as if it’s live. There’s a lot that can be
done around that. There is a gray area where it becomes pretty deceptive. If you’ve
watched enough of my stuff, you know I get squeamish about being deceptive.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 8 --

8
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
When I’ve done these, I will often go with the live webinars, especially if you are
doing them on a rolling launch like a JV Launch. You have to watch. It could be a
slippery slope. You do not want to lie to people. They can find out easily when it is just a
recorded video.
This model does work well. In here, you send them to the sales page but you also
have a replay. They’re on your list. They get squeezed onto a list. After the webinar, you
mail people the replay.
The replay goes back to the sales letter. If they didn’t show up or even if they did,
you’re e-mailing the equivalent of your launch list. You’ve squeezed them onto your
launch list or a sub list.
You’re mailing all these e-mails and driving them to the webinar. If they don’t
make it to the webinar, or even if they do, you mail them the replay. The replay goes right
into the sales page. That’s the webinar based way.
In the next video, I want to talk more about the weekly Evergreen Launch. I have
some pretty cool stuff for that.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
